name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 37
units: metres below ground level; typed Wimpey Geotech / BGS log, description verbatim

intervals.verbatim: Firm mottled dark brown and light grey very sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel sized fragments of coal and sandstone
annotations: gravel-sized fragments of coal and sandstone

intervals.verbatim: Brown becoming dark brown slightly becoming very clayey silty fine to coarse SAND with much becoming some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel. Coal dust throughout

Firm becoming firm to stiff dark greyish brown laminated slightly sandy silty CLAY with some thin bands of light brown fine sand and dark brown medium sand and some fine to medium subangular and subrounded gravel (Glacial Till)
Dense dark brown very clayey very silty fine bound SAND with some fine to medium subangular and subrounded gravel (very sandy Glacial Till)
annotations: logged as very sandy Glacial Till

intervals.verbatim: Dense orangeish brown and brown fine to coarse SAND and fine to coarse subangular and subrounded GRAVEL. Some pockets of dark brown very clayey very silty fine sand near top
annotations: base of superficial deposits; end of cable percussion at 10.30m (DRY)

intervals.verbatim: White moderately discoloured orange fine to medium occasionally fine grained slightly weathered planar bedded occasionally faintly laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong and strong to very strong. Occasional thin beds with very closely spaced weathered coal laminae. Very occasional thin moderately weak beds. Bed of orange moderately becoming highly weathered becoming weak sandstone from 12.20m to 12.55m. Closely occasionally very closely occasionally medium spaced 5 degrees to 20 degrees bedding fractures. Occasional subvertical usually ironstained fractures
annotations: top of rockhead / start of rotary core drilling (25/4/90)
with water: ground-water encountered at 16.00m (GWe in water-depth column)
single logged unit spanning core-run boundaries 13.50/16.50/19.50/22.50m

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine grained moderately discoloured brown moderately and moderately to highly weathered thinly planar laminated very silty SANDSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong and moderately weak. Many thin carbonaceous laminae. Completely discoloured orange and highly fractured below 25.30m. Extremely closely becoming closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures
annotations: completely discoloured orange and highly fractured below 25.30m

intervals.verbatim: White fine to medium grained slightly weathered bedded SANDSTONE - strong. Occasional small carbonaceous traces. Closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures and occasional 70 degrees to subvertical fractures

Orange medium becoming coarse grained moderately weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Many thin laminae and isolated traces of coal. Closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures, locally highly fractured. Completely weathered from 26.85m to 26.95m

Orange fine grained highly discoloured slightly weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong to very strong becoming moderately strong to strong. Close to medium spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures
White becoming pale grey medium becoming fine grained slightly weathered bedded SANDSTONE - strong becoming very strong. Very closely becoming medium spaced 10 degrees to 20 degrees bedding fractures

Pale grey fine to medium grained locally discoloured yellow and orange moderately and moderately to highly weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong and moderately strong. Common small carbonaceous traces. Very closely and closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures. Locally highly fractured. Silty clay infill in some fractures
annotations: silty clay infill in some fractures

intervals.verbatim: White fine grained slightly weathered faintly convolute laminated silty SANDSTONE - strong and moderately strong to strong. Many thin (up to 3mm) coal laminae. Bed of conglomorate containing medium gravel sized commonly elongate siltstone clasts from 31.45m to 31.60m. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding fractures
annotations: conglomerate bed with elongate siltstone clasts 31.45m-31.60m

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured COAL - very weak. Orange ironstained subhorizontal and subvertical fractures
Dark grey fine grained moderately weathered very highly fractured silty MUDSTONE - moderately weak and weak. Many carbonaceous plant fossils. Siltstone from 33.50m to 33.85m
Pale grey fine grained slightly weathered bedded SANDSTONE - strong. Closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Occasional dark siltstone laminae

notes: Modern typed metric log (Wimpey Geotech, Fig. 41), 4 sheets, BGS Record of BOREHOLE 37, A167 Durham Western Bypass. Ground level 82.20 m OD; datum m OD as written. Cable percussion to 10.30m (drift), rotary core drilling 10.30m-34.50m (Carboniferous Coal Measures: sandstone-dominated with one thin 0.50m unnamed coal at 31.60-32.10m over a mudstone). All depths are base-below-ground; every printed OD Level checks exactly against 82.20-depth (no clerk arithmetic slips). The thick interval 7 (10.30-24.10m white sandstone) is a single logged unit spanning four core runs; boundary depths there are core-run marks, not bed changes. On page 4 a depth-column rule at 32.00m carries no OD tick - it is a fracture-log sub-boundary, not a lithological boundary. Original spellings preserved verbatim: 'orangeish', 'conglomorate'. Reference is hand-written on every sheet as the scribal variant 'N224SE/189' (official BGS reference NZ24SE189). Coal is unnamed in the original and no pencil seam correlations are present, so seam_marks is empty. Remarks (sheet 4): 'The borehole was advanced by chiselling from 4.00m to 4.20m (1h) and 8.70m to 10.00m (1.5h). Ground-water was encountered at a depth of 16.00m. Full return of circulation air during rotary drilling. The borehole was backfilled with bentonite/cement grout.'
